name: HMDA LAR Compliance Checker MCP (Clarid AI)
what_it_does: MCP server for validating HMDA (Home Mortgage Disclosure Act) LAR (Loan Application Register) files against CFPB edit checks. Built for community banks and credit unions needing AI-assisted HMDA compliance validation — checking LAR data for regulatory errors before filing.
use_cases: Validating HMDA LAR files against CFPB edit checks from compliance automation agents
Detecting HMDA data errors and inconsistencies from banking compliance agents
Preparing HMDA filings with pre-submission quality checks from regulatory reporting agents
Building compliance workflows for community banks and credit unions using AI
not_for: Non-US mortgage lenders (HMDA is US-specific CFPB regulation)
Large institutions with dedicated HMDA compliance platforms
General loan processing automation beyond HMDA validation
best_when: A community bank or credit union compliance team wants AI agents to validate HMDA LAR data before filing — catching edit check errors and data quality issues without manually running CFPB tools.
avoid_when: You don't file HMDA data. HMDA reporting is required only for covered mortgage lenders. Non-mortgage lending has no HMDA obligation.

auth.notes: Local compliance checker — no external auth required for local deployment. HMDA LAR data is sensitive; run locally or in secure environment.

pricing.notes: Free open source compliance tool. CFPB HMDA edit checks are based on public regulatory specifications.

known_agent_gotchas: PRIVACY: HMDA LAR data contains sensitive personal financial and demographic information
CFPB edit check rules change annually — verify tool is updated for current filing year
HMDA validation is advisory — human compliance officer must review results before filing
LAR data format changes between years — validate input format compatibility
Community implementation — verify against official CFPB HMDA Filing Instructions Guide
agent_readiness.security_notes: PRIVACY: HMDA LAR data includes PII (names, addresses, demographics, financials). Must run in secure environment. Local operation preferred. Compliance results require human expert review.

security_score_components.security_notes: HMDA data is sensitive PII. Local-only operation preferred. No external data transmission. Annual CFPB rule updates required.
